title: Missing Switching canonical identity anchor
description: docs/project_control_board.md and the active Switching workstream row both reference tables/switching_canonical_identity.csv as the intended canonical identity anchor, but the file is absent in the repository. This leaves canonical identity discoverable only indirectly and breaks the documented anchor linkage for current Switching canonical evidence.

title: Query system consumes stale X claim context without canonical-current gate
description: analysis/query/query_system.m prefers an external context bundle path before docs/context_bundle.json and seeds Switching answers from X_canonical_coordinate, X_scaling_relation, and related X_* claims. The control board says query_system is not yet canonical-aware, and the Switching workstream still reports claims_alignment=LEGACY_WEIGHTED, snapshot_alignment=PARTIAL, and query_alignment=NO, so stale or out-of-repo context can be surfaced as if it were current canonical Switching evidence.

title: Repo context bundle and repo state still present width-based X claims as supported current
description: claims/X_canonical_coordinate.json now labels the width-based X claim as LEGACY_NON_CANONICAL_ADVISORY, but docs/context_bundle.json still lists X_canonical_coordinate and related X_* claims as supported/high-confidence and docs/repo_state.json still presents X as the Switching bridge observable. Required read-before-work context therefore still flattens legacy-advisory width-based claims into current-looking Switching guidance.

title: Snapshot claim status maps still rate width-based X claims as strong current
description: snapshot_scientific_v3/60_claims_surveys/proven_status_map.json still rates X_canonical_coordinate and X_scaling_relation as strong, and snapshot_scientific_v3/70_evidence_index/evidence_summary.json still summarizes the same width-based X claim stack as active evidence. The control board says snapshot_scientific_v3 may lag canonical integration unless snapshot_alignment=YES, but the Switching workstream still reports snapshot_alignment=PARTIAL.
